meta_title: For Students: client workflow to Solve can't finish projects (Beginner Resource Page)
meta_description: Learn how to client workflow and overcome can't finish projects. A Beginner-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Finish Your First AI App: The Student Client Workflow Guide
content.seo_description: Stop abandoning projects. Master a professional client workflow using Antigravity and Claude Code to launch your first real-world AI application today.
content.answer_block: The secret to finishing projects isn't talent; it's a rigid client-side workflow. By decoupling your UI from backend logic using Antigravity and automating development with Claude Code, you eliminate scope creep. Stop building 'everything' and start shipping specific, functional features that compound into a portfolio-ready SaaS application.
content.intro:
The graveyard of unfinished side projects is paved with good intentions and broken momentum. You start with a spark, build a chaotic prototype, and then hit the wall: feature creep, confusing state management, and the paralyzing realization that you don't know how to finish. It’s not that you lack the skill; you lack a system.

Professional developers don't 'just code'; they operate within a defined workflow that forces completion. By treating your development process like a client project—with strict constraints and automated scaffolding—you move from a student struggling with syntax to a builder shipping production-ready code. It’s time to stop tinkering and start launching.

steps.title: Define the Minimum Viable Scope
steps.description: Before writing code, define the exact feature set that constitutes 'done' to prevent infinite feature expansion.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a Senior Product Manager. Review my app idea and create a list of exactly 3 core features that make this a functional MVP. Exclude all 'nice-to-have' extras.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Generate a project spec file based on these 3 core features.'

steps.title: Bootstrap with Antigravity Architecture
steps.description: Use Antigravity to generate a clean, modular folder structure that separates your logic from your UI.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a scalable Next.js directory structure using the Clean Architecture pattern, specifically optimized for an AI-integrated SaaS application.

steps.title: Automate UI Development
steps.description: Use Claude Code to translate your UI components from your Antigravity schema into responsive, reusable React code.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Based on the schema generated in step 2, write the primary dashboard component with clean Tailwind CSS styling.
steps.claude_code_command: claude code 'Implement the dashboard component using the provided schema and Tailwind CSS.'

steps.title: Implement API Integration Logic
steps.description: Establish a robust connection between your client and your AI backend to ensure seamless data flow.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a service layer function in TypeScript that handles the API request to the AI backend with error handling and loading states.
steps.claude_code_command: claude code 'Generate the API service layer and add robust error handling for the AI response.'

steps.title: Final Deployment and Validation
steps.description: Run a final build check and deploy your project, turning your code into a live, portfolio-ready URL.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Run a static analysis of my current codebase and suggest any final optimizations before production deployment.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'npm run build && vercel deploy'

pro_tips: Always commit your code after every single successful feature test to maintain a psychological feedback loop of winning.
Use Claude Code to document your code as you write; it forces you to explain the logic, which exposes holes in your understanding.
Limit your development sessions to 90 minutes. If you can't finish a feature in that window, your feature is too big—break it down further.
Treat the 'Console' as your best friend; if you aren't logging your data flow, you are flying blind.
Never start a new feature until the current one is deployed and tested in a live browser environment.
content.next_steps: Once your first project is live, share the link in our community Slack to receive peer code reviews and start your second, more complex iteration.
content.conversion_block: You now have the framework to stop starting and start shipping. Stop letting your ideas die in a private repository. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.

acceptedAnswer.text: Before writing code, define the exact feature set that constitutes 'done' to prevent infinite feature expansion.
Use Antigravity to generate a clean, modular folder structure that separates your logic from your UI.
Use Claude Code to translate your UI components from your Antigravity schema into responsive, reusable React code.
Establish a robust connection between your client and your AI backend to ensure seamless data flow.
Run a final build check and deploy your project, turning your code into a live, portfolio-ready URL.
